task: Digital Fortress Atomic Staging: 1) Write src/middleware/fortress.js (CommonJS) exporting: (a) Express middleware setting common security headers and adding Strict-Transport-Security only if process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true'; (b) async Koa middleware (ctx, next) mirroring same headers; (c) Express Router named router with GET /status/fo returning JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }. 2) Update src/server.js to use Express, apply the fortress Express middleware and mount router. 3) Run `npm test` and verify tests pass. Ensure src/middleware directory exists (create if missing). Atomic staging; no revert.

subtasks.description: Create CommonJS module src/middleware/fortress.js exporting: expressMiddleware (sets security headers; HSTS only if process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true'), async koaMiddleware with same headers, and an Express router with GET /status/fo returning { status: 'ok', fortress: true }.
subtasks.test_command: node -e "const m=require('./src/middleware/fortress.js'); if(!m||typeof m.expressMiddleware!=='function'||typeof m.koaMiddleware!=='function'||!m.router){console.error('exports missing'); process.exit(1);} console.log('ok');"
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: src/middleware/fortress.js exists and is valid CommonJS; exports include expressMiddleware, koaMiddleware, and router. expressMiddleware sets CSP, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, COOP, COEP, and sets Strict-Transport-Security only when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS==='true'. koaMiddleware mirrors headers and awaits next(). router handles GET /status/fo with JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }.

changes.explanation: Add new CommonJS module implementing security middlewares for Express and Koa and an Express router endpoint per the subtask.

subtasks.description: Update src/server.js to use Express, apply the fortress Express middleware, and mount the fortress router so /status/fo works. Prefer CommonJS require. Do not start the server when required; only listen if run directly.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: src/server.js requires Express and creates/uses an Express app. It requires ./middleware/fortress.js and calls app.use(expressMiddleware) and app.use(router). Route GET /status/fo responds with JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }. The server does not automatically start on require (listens only if run directly) and npm test passes.

plan_summary: Add a security middleware module for Express and Koa, then wire it into the Express server. Create src/middleware/fortress.js exporting expressMiddleware, koaMiddleware, and an Express router with /status/fo. Update src/server.js to use Express, apply the middleware, and mount the router. Ensure the middleware directory exists and run npm tests.
risk_assessment: Medium -- Potential mismatches with existing server.js (ESM vs CommonJS) or different app structure might require careful integration. Starting the server during tests could cause hangs; ensure listen is gated behind require.main check. Missing Express dependency could fail tests if not already installed.
